title: Iran's Women's Football Team Needs Safety Help After Asian Cup
summary: In Australia, people are worried about the safety of Iran's women's football team after they lost in the Asian Cup. The team, called the Iranian Lionesses, did not sing their national anthem in their first game, which made some people in Iran angry. Supporters in Australia cheered for the team and booed during the anthem because they do not like Iran's current government. They waved an old flag from before Iran's revolution. A former Australian football captain, Craig Foster, says the team should be kept safe because they played in a tournament. The team sang the anthem in later games, and some think they were forced to do it. Fans showed support, but the players could not talk much because they might be in danger when they go back to Iran.

background_read: This article is about Iran's women's football team playing in the Asian Cup in Australia. After they lost, people are asking for help to keep them safe because they did not sing Iran's national anthem in one game, which made some people in Iran angry. Supporters in Australia showed they care by cheering and waving an old flag. The team might face problems when they return to Iran, so leaders like Craig Foster want to protect them.
Article_Structure: WHO: Iran's women's football team, supporters in Australia, Craig Foster
WHAT: People are urging for the team's safety after they did not sing the national anthem and lost in the Asian Cup
WHERE: In Australia, at the Asian Cup tournament
WHY: Because the team might be in danger when they go back to Iran after their actions

perspectives.description: People worry the team could be punished in Iran for not singing the anthem.
Australian supporters showed love by booing the anthem and waving an old flag.
